searchKeyword.title: Search Keyword
searchKeyword.description: The primary keyword to search for in LinkedIn posts. This is combined with each job role to form search queries.
searchKeyword.sectionDescription: Configure what kind of posts you want to find on LinkedIn.

jobRoles.title: Job Roles
jobRoles.description: List of job roles/titles to search for. Each role generates a separate search combined with the search keyword. Example: Product Designer, UI/UX Designer, Frontend Developer

location.title: Location
location.description: Filter posts by location. Use a city, country, or region name (e.g., 'India', 'United States', 'San Francisco', 'London'). Leave empty for worldwide results.
location.sectionDescription: Narrow down results by location and recency.

datePosted.title: Date Posted
datePosted.description: Filter posts by when they were published.

maxResults.title: Max Results
maxResults.description: Maximum number of posts to collect per job role. LinkedIn limits search results, so very high numbers may not yield more results.

li_at.title: LinkedIn Session Cookie (li_at)
li_at.description: Your LinkedIn 'li_at' session cookie. To get it: 1) Log into LinkedIn in your browser, 2) Open DevTools (F12), 3) Go to Application > Cookies > linkedin.com, 4) Copy the value of the 'li_at' cookie.
li_at.sectionDescription: LinkedIn requires authentication to access post search results. Provide your session cookie below.

proxyConfig.title: Proxy Configuration
proxyConfig.description: Select proxies to use. Residential proxies are strongly recommended for LinkedIn to avoid blocks.
